chunk_summary: Clinical vignettes address risks in vegetarian school-age and adolescent children, focusing on protein, iron, calcium, and vitamin D deficiencies, alongside feeding patterns and fasting practices.
learning_objectives: Identify nutritional risks in vegetarian school-age and adolescent children.
Develop tailored dietary interventions for protein, iron, and calcium deficiencies.
Understand the impact of regional feeding patterns and fasting practices on child growth and health.
key_facts: Protein anchors should be included in all main meals (e.g., dal, tofu, paneer).
Vitamin D deficiency may require supplementation and monitoring of calcium, phosphate, alkaline phosphatase, and 25(OH)D levels.
Iron deficiency anemia should be treated with standard elemental iron dosing and continued beyond hemoglobin normalization.
Children should not follow adult-style fasting; protein must be included during fasting days.
Screen for feeding disorders in cases of selective eating or faltering growth.
reference_quote: Children should not follow adult-style fasting; if fasting continues, protein must be included (milk/curd, soy/tofu, legumes) and breakfast should not be skipped. — IAP parental guidelines (Nutrition 5–12 Years)
document_key_topics: Impact of fasting on child nutrition
